laws.title: Public Law 2-12 | Fee Prohibition Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Federal courts may not impose, collect, or otherwise require fees in proceedings unless the fee is imposed as punishment for a criminal offense.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; no self-repeal, sunset, or later-law repeal was identified in the reviewed materials.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal or transfer issue; the fee bar is a live Title 28 rule and belongs in operative text.
laws.rationale: This is a permanent substantive court-fee rule. A note alone does not capture the operative prohibition, and the current implementation is not legally sufficient.

laws.title: Public Law 2-13 | The Employees Termination Notification Act of 2021
laws.final_statutory_text: Employers covered by the act must notify an employee of termination and provide the reason within 48 hours.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; no self-repeal, sunset, or later-law repeal was identified in the reviewed materials.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal or transfer issue; the employment-notice rule remains live and belongs in operative text.
laws.rationale: The act states a continuing employment-notice rule. Historical-note treatment is not enough because the source creates substantive duties.

laws.title: Public Law 2-14 | National Federal Torts Act
laws.final_statutory_text: The act creates federal civil jurisdiction for recognized tort claims, defines multiple civil torts, and sets government-liability and false-arrest relief rules.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; no self-repeal, sunset, or later-law repeal was identified in the reviewed materials.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal or transfer issue; the tort and jurisdiction framework remains live and requires operative codification.
laws.rationale: This is a broad substantive civil-law framework. The current note-only treatment omits the operative jurisdiction, tort, and government-liability rules.

laws.title: Public Law 3-15 | Admission to the United Nations Act
laws.final_statutory_text: The President must notify Congress and send a letter of consent or denial concerning admission to the United Nations.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; no self-repeal, sunset, or later-law repeal was identified in the reviewed materials.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal or transfer issue; the notification and consent procedure remains live and requires operative text.
laws.rationale: The source creates a continuing federal procedure. A statutory note is not a sufficient substitute for the operative Title 44 reporting and consent language.

laws.title: Public Law 3-16 | The Capitol Police Establishment
laws.final_statutory_text: The act establishes the Capitol Police Board, protective powers, arrest authority, Capitol-complex policing duties, and outside-jurisdiction deployment rules.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; no self-repeal, sunset, or later-law repeal was identified in the reviewed materials.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal or transfer issue; the Capitol Police framework remains live and belongs in operative Title 2 text.
laws.rationale: This is a permanent legislative-branch police framework. The note-only implementation does not preserve the operative authorities created by the act.

laws.title: Public Law 3-17 | The Diplomatic Protections Act
laws.final_statutory_text: The act defines foreign diplomats, requires State Department protection, authorizes removal and deportation, grants immunity when authorized, and sets a 24-hour departure timetable.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; no self-repeal, sunset, or later-law repeal was identified in the reviewed materials.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal or transfer issue; the diplomatic-protection rules remain live and require operative Title 22 text.
laws.rationale: The act is a continuing substantive diplomatic rule. Note treatment is not legally sufficient for the protection, immunity, and departure provisions.

laws.title: Public Law 3-18 | Amendment to S.12
laws.final_statutory_text: Section V of the Capitol Police enactment is replaced with new outside-jurisdiction authority, including D.C. law enforcement, emergency response, intelligence gathering, and protective services.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; it expressly amends PL 3-16 Section V and contains no self-repeal or sunset.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: The source performs a direct replacement of existing Section V rather than creating a separate standalone framework.
laws.rationale: This is a direct amendment to the Capitol Police statute. The correct treatment is to amend the existing text, not to leave the change as a historical note.

laws.title: Public Law 3-19 | The NARA Act
laws.final_statutory_text: The President must notify the Archivist of the United States of all bills signed into law.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; no self-repeal, sunset, or later-law repeal was identified in the reviewed materials.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal or transfer issue; the Archivist-notification rule remains live and belongs in operative text.
laws.rationale: The act creates a continuing federal reporting duty. A note-only placement does not satisfy the codification required by the source.

laws.title: Public Law 3-20 | National Immigration Employment Act of 2021
laws.final_statutory_text: Source text could not be recovered from the supplied materials.
laws.chronology: Unresolved source; chronology cannot be assessed without recoverable operative text.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Unsupported because the underlying statutory text is unavailable.
laws.rationale: The batch supplies no extractable operative text, so the enactment cannot be verified or codified from the reviewed materials.

laws.title: Public Law 3-21 | Effective Judicial Function and General Duties Act
laws.final_statutory_text: The Chief Justice must ensure prompt court business and submit reports to the Judiciary Committees, the President, and the Judicial Conference.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; no self-repeal, sunset, or superseding clause was identified in the reviewed materials.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal or transfer issue; the judicial-administration duties remain live and require operative Title 28 text.
laws.rationale: This is a continuing judicial-administration statute. The current note-only treatment is legally insufficient because the act creates operative duties and reporting obligations.
